This is a brief in opposition to a petition for certiorari in Zook v. Fuqua (No. 25-1108), a Section 1983 qualified-immunity excessive-force case from the Tenth Circuit. Summarize: (1) the respondent's main arguments against certiorari (vehicle problems, split denial, fact-bound arguments); (2) how respondent characterizes the alleged circuit split on considering video evidence at the motion-to-dismiss stage; (3) any argument about the interlocutory posture; (4) anything else notable.

uv run python - <<'EOF'
import sys
try:
    from pypdf import PdfReader
except ImportError:
    try:
        from PyPDF2 import PdfReader
    except ImportError:
        sys.exit("no pdf lib")
r = PdfReader('/tmp/bio.pdf')
print("pages:", len(r.pages))
text = "\n".join(p.extract_text() or "" for p in r.pages)
open('/tmp/bio.txt','w').write(text)
print(text[:3000])
EOF
